explanation: This requirement means that whenever prop_1 occurs, prop_0 must also occur (at the same time). In LTL, it is common to express this as: globally, whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold.
Whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 should also hold at the same time. This is captured by the globally operator encapsulating the implication from prop_1 to prop_0.

The sentence means 'prop_0 happens whenever prop_1 happens.' In LTL, this is represented by 'Globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 is also true.' This ensures that at every moment where prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold.
Whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold at the same time. That is, prop_0 is required whenever prop_1 is true.
This specifies that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 should also hold at that moment. In LTL, this is generally represented by the formula: globally, if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 must also hold.
Whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 should also be true at the same time. This expresses an instantaneous response: when prop_1 holds, prop_0 must hold as well.
Whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold at the same moment. This can be captured as: at all times, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 is also true.
Whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must be true at the same time. In other words, always, if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 holds.
Whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold at the same time. This is best captured by the formula: Globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true.
The statement means that whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 should also hold at the same instant. This is a standard implication capturing that prop_0 must be true whenever prop_1 is true.
